diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index b5ce4b6..cfce6c6 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -126,2 +126,8 @@ Week 19 / day 126 observations:
 - Next billing window is weaker, roughly $36k against weekly costs around $47k, so do not spend the new cushion. Hold prices, quotas, tier 3 capacity, C tier 4, no ads/dev, and $2.5k/day total ops with S2 targeting.
 - Repeated $9 E3 rescue offers again. These leads are effectively optional upside after many non-converting turns.
+
+Week 20 / day 133 observations:
+- Cash fell to $43.0k on the weaker billing window. Cancellations rose to 67 and open issues jumped to 527, while MRR remains about $192k due C upgrades.
+- Current churn is not catastrophic in MRR terms, but support/reputation risk is compounding. Many S2 tickets are now 60-80 days old, and social continues to cite support and quota ceilings.
+- Week 20 action: keep prices, quotas, tier 3 capacity, C tier 4, no ads/dev, but raise S2 targeted ops from $2k/day to $3k/day for one week while keeping global ops at $500/day.
+- Repeated $9 E3 rescue offers; no further spend should be justified by enterprise unless a deal actually closes.
